attacking_style: Launches decisive moves on classified climbs with 35-45km remaining, preferring solo efforts from breakaways rather than late-stage punches. Lacks explosive acceleration to counter stronger closers in final kilometers.
team_tactics: Operates in dual role: executes extended domestique shifts for team leaders (30km+ pulls documented) but capitalizes on breakaway freedom when GC ambitions are off the table. UAE relies heavily on him when squad is depleted.
terrain_preference: Classified climbs and descents where positioning and patience trump pure power. Excels in long-range solo efforts requiring sustained tempo rather than repeated accelerations. Vulnerable in echelon-prone flat stages and crash-heavy bunch finishes.
vulnerability: Poor positioning instincts in nervous stages—caught in echelon splits and crashed outside 3km protection. Mechanical issues and lack of finishing punch against stronger closers consistently undermine GC campaigns.
key_patterns: Wins from breakaways via long-range attacks on climbs (35-45km out), not late accelerations. Two wins documented.
Positioning errors cost significant GC time: echelon split (-2:05), crash outside 3km protection. Multiple instances.
Transitions effectively between domestique duties and personal opportunities, recognizing tactical moments to counter-attack from service role.
Demonstrates patience and tactical calculation over raw power: bridged on descent, sustained solo efforts, calculated chases.
Exposed when UAE squad is depleted—both as suffering rider and as overextended domestique. Team depth critical.
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 GT stage win shows tactical maturity, but GC losses from positioning errors persist unchanged from 2021-2022 pattern
